key_insight: E^3RL uses the model’s own local cross-entropy as an intrinsic uncertainty signal to detect and erase early reasoning defects, helping long-horizon LLM reasoning recover instead of cascading into failure.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical self-healing RL design for LLMs that preserves KV-cache reuse, maintains linear memory overhead, and improves sample efficiency for long-sequence reasoning systems.
method: Define epistemic entropy from endogenous autoregressive cross-entropy instead of external rewards alone
Apply segment-level adaptive thresholds and advantage allocation to localize and excise faulty reasoning spans
Reuse historical KV-cache streams to repair trajectories without restarting generation
evidence_signal: The paper trains on DeepMath-103k and reports strong gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarks such as AIME, with 4B and 8B models exceeding prior SOTA by 5.349% and 6.514% respectively.
watch_item: Performance and stability may depend on how well the adaptive thresholding and cache-reuse mechanism generalize across tasks and reasoning lengths beyond the evaluated math benchmarks.
paper_title: Shattering the Autoregressive Curse: Dynamic Epistemic Entropy Orchestrated Erasable Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
